author: Alex Rivera
text: The internet gave everyone access to culture, but it removed a lot of the structures that helped people understand it.

drafts.reply_1: Access expanded faster than understanding. Rebuilding context feels like one of the most important digital problems now.
drafts.reply_2: The archive got bigger. The map got weaker. That tension feels central to digital culture.
drafts.quote: A lot of what interests me sits exactly here: not just access to culture, but better context around it.
drafts.new_post: The internet made culture easier to reach, but harder to situate. Maybe the next layer is context, not just access.

author: Design Thread
text: A lot of digital products don’t have a design problem. They have a meaning problem.

drafts.reply_1: Access expanded faster than understanding. Rebuilding context feels like one of the most important digital problems now.
drafts.reply_2: The archive got bigger. The map got weaker. That tension feels central to digital culture.
drafts.quote: A lot of what interests me sits exactly here: not just access to culture, but better context around it.
drafts.new_post: The internet made culture easier to reach, but harder to situate. Maybe the next layer is context, not just access.

author: Museum Lens
text: Museums should stop pretending to be neutral institutions.

drafts.reply_1: That tension is part of what makes institutions worth examining: they don’t just preserve culture, they frame it.
drafts.reply_2: Neutrality in cultural institutions has always been more complicated than it looks.
drafts.quote: What interests me here is not just the institution, but the way meaning gets shaped through selection, display and context.
drafts.new_post: Museums are not only archives. They are systems of interpretation.

author: Mina Cole
text: Most people don’t need more ideas. They need better systems.

drafts.reply_1: Strong ideas usually collapse inside weak systems.
drafts.reply_2: A lot of creative work is really structure in disguise.
drafts.quote: The gap between a good idea and a useful project is often system design.
drafts.new_post: The internet doesn’t lack ideas. It lacks structure.
